title: Daily Market Digest: June 29, 2026
subtitle: US Futures Rise on Geopolitical De-escalation; Tech Sector Under Pressure

items.headline: Futures Climb as US-Iran Ceasefire Hopes Boost Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. stock futures advanced Monday morning, driven by reports of a ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, which is expected to stabilize oil prices and temper recent market volatility. This positive geopolitical development is helping to offset lingering concerns over inflation and potential interest rate hikes. While Friday saw a mixed close with tech weakness globally, Monday's pre-market activity reflects a cautious return of risk appetite. Consumer sentiment, as measured by the University of Michigan, was revised up slightly to 49.5 in June, though still below the 50-point mark.

items.headline: Top Gainer
items.summary: Viridian Therapeutics surged in premarket trading after announcing U.S. FDA approval and launch of Lumvoa for thyroid eye disease.

items.headline: Notable Gainer
items.summary: BlackBerry traded higher in Monday's premarket, building on its best week in over two years following strong quarterly results that outperformed expectations.

items.headline: Tech Sector Weakness
items.summary: The broader technology sector continues to experience rotation and weakness, especially among mega-cap and AI-linked stocks, due to concerns over memory prices and the sustainability of the AI rally. Individual chipmakers and software companies saw declines last week.

items.headline: Notable Decliner (Recent)
items.summary: SpaceX experienced a significant sell-off last week, testing below $150, following news of a $20 billion bond sale.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Set for Holiday-Shortened Week
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting a slate of U.S. labor market data in this holiday-shortened week, with markets closed Friday for Independence Day. Key releases include June consumer confidence and JOLTS job openings on Tuesday, ADP employment and ISM Manufacturing PMI on Wednesday, and the pivotal June Non-Farm Payrolls report on Thursday. The Non-Farm Payrolls report, in particular, will be scrutinized for clues on Fed policy.

items.headline: Fed's Hawkish Stance on Inflation Persists Amid Rate Hike Speculation
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's commitment to price stability remains firm, with the May Core PCE inflation rising to 3.4% year-on-year, the fastest pace since October 2023. New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh has emphasized bringing inflation back to the 2% target, leading to increased speculation of a September rate hike. However, market odds for a near-term hike were somewhat scaled back after recent PCE inflation data aligned with expectations and oil prices fell.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues: Defensives Outperform Tech
items.summary: The market is witnessing a continued rotation out of mega-cap and AI-linked technology stocks, which have seen significant pressure recently, into more defensive sectors. Healthcare, Utilities, and Real Estate were among the leading sectors last week, with Utilities and Real Estate improving their momentum. Consumer Discretionary and Consumer Staples also retain leadership, reflecting investor confidence in consumer spending.

items.headline: Nike Earnings Headline a Light Earnings Week
items.summary: Nike Inc. ($NKE) is a key company reporting earnings this week, with its release scheduled for Tuesday, June 30, after market close. Analysts anticipate a revenue decline of approximately 3% to $10.85 billion and EPS near $0.12. Investors will be watching for insights into margin recovery, inventory management, and performance in key markets like Greater China.
